message:
perf(storage): scan idx_issues_created_at for default ready high-bucket query

The default `br ready` query (no filters, oldest-first ordering, a
limit, and the implicit hybrid high-bucket priority filter that drops
P2/P3 work) is the single hottest read path on this storage layer:
every agent invokes it dozens of times per session to pick up its
next piece of work.

That query was previously planned as a full `FROM issues WHERE 1=1`
scan with the priority IN (...) and status filters applied, then
sorted by created_at to pick the oldest. SQLite has the right index
(`idx_issues_created_at`) but the planner doesn't reliably choose it
because the WHERE clause includes equality filters on indexed
columns (status, priority) that look more selective on paper —
even though for a typical agent project, almost all open issues land
in the high bucket and the created_at ordering is what actually
prunes the result set down to the limit.

Adds two helpers:

- `priorities_cover_ready_high_bucket(priorities)` — returns true iff
  the priorities slice is exactly `[CRITICAL, HIGH]` (order
  irrelevant, length pinned at 2). This is the implicit priority
  shape that the hybrid-high-bucket helper produces when the user
  doesn't override `--priority`. Anything else (including supersets
  like `[CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM]`) falls through to the existing
  planner path because the created_at scan stops being a clear win
  once medium+ buckets are eligible.

- `should_scan_ready_by_created_at(filters, sort)` — gate that
  decides whether to use the indexed-scan path. Requires the
  high-bucket priority shape AND oldest-first sort AND none of the
  filters that would force a join or extra constraint:
  `include_deferred=false`, `unassigned=false`, no labels_and /
  labels_or, no `types`, no `assignee`, no `parent`. With those
  preconditions, the resulting query is exactly `SELECT ... FROM
  issues INDEXED BY idx_issues_created_at WHERE 1=1` plus the usual
  status / priority / limit clauses appended below — SQLite walks
  the created_at index in order and stops at the limit.

The `INDEXED BY` hint pins the planner to `idx_issues_created_at`
even when its statistics suggest other indexes look more selective,
because we know empirically that for the default ready query, the
ordered scan terminates earlier than any of the alternatives.

Test (`test_ready_high_bucket_query_uses_created_at_index_for_default_limited_hybrid`)
confirms two things:

1. The default high-bucket filter set with a limit produces a SQL
   string containing `FROM issues INDEXED BY idx_issues_created_at`.
2. Adding ANY filter outside the allow-list (in this case `assignee`)
   flips `should_scan_ready_by_created_at` back to false so the
   normal planner path takes over.

Test also validates the parameter count stays at 2 (the priority
list), confirming no extra bound parameters are accidentally emitted
by the new branch.
